Transaction ID.semantic_meaning: Unique identifier for each sales transaction/order record.
Transaction ID.potential_issues: Appears unique and sequential, which is appropriate for an ID. Stored as int64, but should not be used for numeric analysis. If IDs can contain leading zeros in other systems, integer storage could be problematic.

Date.semantic_meaning: Date on which the transaction or sale occurred.
Date.potential_issues: Values appear consistently formatted as YYYY-MM-DD, but dtype is string rather than a parsed date type. Should be converted to a proper datetime field for time-series analysis.

Product Category.semantic_meaning: High-level product classification used to group items into business categories.
Product Category.potential_issues: Sample values look clean and standardized. Possible issue is category overlap or inconsistent assignment between Product Name and Product Category, which cannot be verified from samples alone.

Product Name.semantic_meaning: Specific product/item sold in the transaction.
Product Name.potential_issues: High-cardinality product labels with brand/model text. Potential issues include inconsistent naming conventions, punctuation differences, duplicates under slightly different names, and lack of a separate product SKU/master ID.

Units Sold.semantic_meaning: Quantity of units purchased in the transaction line.
Units Sold.potential_issues: Values are positive integers and look reasonable. Should be validated for zero, negative, or unusually large quantities outside the sample.

Unit Price.semantic_meaning: Selling price per individual unit of the product at the time of transaction.
Unit Price.potential_issues: Values appear plausible and monetary. As a float, it may be subject to rounding/precision issues; fixed decimal/currency type would be preferable. Should also be checked for currency consistency across regions.

Total Revenue.semantic_meaning: Gross revenue generated by the transaction line, likely calculated as Units Sold multiplied by Unit Price.
Total Revenue.potential_issues: Sample values are consistent with Units Sold × Unit Price, which is good. As a float, may have rounding precision issues. Should be validated against source columns for all rows and checked for discounts, taxes, or shipping being excluded/included.

Region.semantic_meaning: Geographic sales region associated with the transaction/customer/order.
Region.potential_issues: Values appear standardized but are very broad (only 3 regions). May be too coarse for detailed geographic analysis, and region definitions may be ambiguous without a country/subregion field.

Payment Method.semantic_meaning: Method used by the customer to pay for the transaction.
Payment Method.potential_issues: Values appear clean and standardized. Limited set is reasonable, but may mask more detailed payment types (e.g., digital wallets, bank transfer). Should be checked for missing or inconsistent labels in full data.
